home.history-text: Founded in 2003 in Île Perrot, Kelly La Solution Inc. is built on over 50 years of expertise in septic systems. The company offers complete services including French drain cleaning, mini-camera inspections, system installation and repair, excavation, and prefilter sales and maintenance. Kelly La Solution stands out for its exclusive soil decompaction process using air injection, a fast and non-invasive method that improves all types of soil without damaging the surface. Due to its success, a franchise opened in Val-Bélair in 2005 to serve the greater Quebec City area.
french-drain-list: Access pit installation for easier maintenance
home.companies-title: We work with homeowners, cities, and municipalities

septic-system.leach-field-text: Specialists in soil aeration and decompaction using innovative air-injection technology to restore septic fields efficiently and sustainably.
septic-system.problem-text: A failing septic system can cause surface water, slow drainage, odors, or backups inside the house.
problem-list: Spongy ground after rain or snowmelt
High water level in the septic tank
septic-system.solution-text: Kelly La Solution Inc. uses an exclusive restoration method that resolves soil compaction and saturation, extending the life of your septic system without damaging landscaping.
septic-system.how-it-works-text: After locating the drain field, a probe is inserted 1–2 meters into the soil. High-pressure air is injected to create pathways for water and oxygen, restoring proper drainage. The process is repeated across the field based on soil conditions.
septic-system.prefilter-text: Prefilters prevent solids from clogging the drain field and require regular maintenance.
septic-system.prefilter-text-under-pic: Prefilters retain grease and suspended solids, extending system life. Cleaning frequency can be adapted to your needs.
septic-system.excavation-subtitle: Mini-excavation and chimney installation to avoid unnecessary digging
septic-system.location-text: We locate septic systems for permits related to pools, property expansion, garages, or sheds—regardless of system age.

french-drain.french-drain-text: Iron ochre problem? We solve iron ochre issues, a viscous substance commonly called iron-eating bacteria. Sometimes, this orange sludge develops in the foundation drain, which can obstruct it and cause damage to the pump. Experts in French drain cleaning and camera inspection, we also install access chimneys and pumping stations.

sport-field.the-problem-text: Sports fields (baseball, football, soccer, volleyball ...), green spaces, dog parks, vineyards ... no matter the nature of the ground, the soil is too compact.
sport-field.symptoms-text: After a rain the water remains on the surface of the ground. The land stays wet for a long time. Drainage no longer works.
sport-field.solution-text: Kelly La Solution Inc. owns an air-injection restoration technique to solve soil problems (including compaction). Specialist in aeration and decompaction of all types of soils. Our technology is: economic, efficient, environmentally friendly and unique.
sport-field.operation-text: Using a pneumatic hammer in the soil we insert a probe of 3 cm in diameter and a depth between 7 and 12 inches. Using a high-pressure compressor, approximately 65 pounds of air are injected in the ground. After the air injection, the ground cracks horizontally and vertically. The surface of the ground will move about 3 meters in diameter, the drainage is instantaneous. This solution is repeated every 1.5 meters according to the soil condition.

q1.question: How can I locate my septic system for a pool permit?
q1.answer: We use a pipe detector to locate your drain field so you can proceed with your permit application.

q2.question: Do you serve my area?
q2.answer: Yes. We operate across Quebec, from Gaspésie to Baie-James.

q3.question: What services do you offer?
q3.answer: Soil aeration, repairs, excavation, installation, inspections, prefilters, and French drain cleaning.

q4.question: When is the best time for work?
q4.answer: We operate from mid-April to mid-December. Contact us early to reserve.

q5.question: My system is clogged—what should I do?
q5.answer: We restore drainage performance using air-injection technology.

q6.question: How long does a repair take?
q6.answer: Most repairs take 4–8 hours and are completed the same day.

q7.question: Will the ground be damaged?
q7.answer: Small holes appear temporarily and disappear within days.

q8.question: Is the process environmentally safe?
q8.answer: Yes. It restores your system to its original function.

q9.question: How can I tell if my system has a problem?
q9.answer: Check water levels, blockages, and surface water on the field.

q10.question: Can I connect a water softener to my system?
q10.answer: No. It creates buildup and excess water in the septic system.
